page: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8573C processor featuring 3 physical cores and 6 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. This server is heavily memory-optimized, providing 252.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 42.0 GB of memory per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities. The server achieves top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression (34.92 GB/sec) and Geekbench single-core testing (2350.0), alongside strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression (14.31 GB/sec). Multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core (6664.64) and Geekbench multi-core (7274.0), fall into the average range. Memory bandwidth is measured at 517.99 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 50.68 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

Due to the absence of local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance is not suited for local-storage-heavy tasks or GPU-accelerated deep learning. Large-model LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with text generation on a medium 7B model performing weakly at 6.65 tokens/sec. However, the combination of a high 4.2 GHz clock speed and 252.0 GB of RAM makes it highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, in-memory caching (such as Redis, which benchmarks at 9.49 million operations/sec), and single-thread-heavy processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 3 physical cores, 6 vCPUs, and a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. The server is characterized by its substantial memory capacity of 252.0 GB, yielding 42.0 GB of RAM per core. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by its Geekbench single-core score of 2350.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 34.92 GB/sec. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive, single-thread-bound applications, making it highly suitable for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise workloads requiring massive RAM allocations relative to compute cores.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 6 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 4.2 GHz processor, and 252 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 6 vCPUs, a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, and 252 GB of RAM for high-memory workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server pairing a high-frequency Intel Xeon processor with an expansive memory footprint for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 6 vCPUs operating at 4.2 GHz
252.0 GB of RAM providing 42.0 GB of memory per core
Top-tier single-core performance in compression and text processing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads
